response: THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², WHERE R IS THE RADIUS. THE RADIUS OF THE 1st CIRCLE IS r, AND EACH NEXT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THE RADIUS OF THE n-th CIRCLE IS r × (1.1)^(n-1). THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR² FOR n FROM 1 TO 8. THIS CAN BE EXPRESSED AS πr² × [1 + 1.1 + 1.1² + 1.1³ + 1.1⁴ + 1.1⁵ + 1.1⁶ + 1.1⁷]. THE TOTAL AREA IS πr² × [1 + 1.1 + 1.1² + 1.1³ + 1.1⁴ + 1.1⁵ + 1.1⁶ + 1.1⁷]. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS CALCULATED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πr × (1.1)^4. THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS IS 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS 2πr × (1.1)^4 / 0.5. THIS IS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE LETTER I APPEARS 11 TIMES IN THIS RESPONSE.
